reforms.description: The Employment Stabilisation Fund was established to promote occupational mobility and provide comprehensive support for unemployment allowances, alongside a law enacted to facilitate the transfer and reemployment of labor from structurally depressed industries, providing support for affected workers.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These reforms aim to enhance labor mobility and support workers, which can lead to improved productivity and economic resilience.
reforms.status_evidence: Both the Employment Stabilisation Fund and the law for labor transfer and reemployment have been established and are in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: While these reforms are significant in supporting labor mobility, they do not represent a structural overhaul of the system.
reforms.source_quote: In October 1977, the 'Employment Adjustment Grant' scheme...was integrated in a permanent and more comprehensive 'Employment Stabilisation Fund' aiming notably at promoting occupational mobility.
